role_title: Senior Technical Program Manager, Information Security

level_signals: 6+ years of experience as a technical program manager in information security
Acts as player-coach to other program managers
Reports into Head of Security Strategy and Governance
Comp range $100K–$150K base, consistent with Senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience as a technical program manager in information security, with a track record of delivering complex, multi-team initiatives across engineering and security stakeholders; demonstrated expertise scoping, planning, and delivering strategic and tactical security program within a matrixed environment

Manage the Information Security Risk Management lifecycle by partnering with engineering and security experts to implement regulations, test controls, and deploy security solutions across the technology stack.
Working knowledge of security frameworks — NIST, ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI DSS, CIS Controls — and how they translate into credible delivery plans
Project management and/or security framework certifications (PMP, PRINCE2, ITIL, COBIT, ISO 27001) are expected at this level
Experience building and tracking security KPIs and metrics to measure program success and drive continuous improvement.

Practical experience using AI-enabled or automation-first program tooling (security GRC, TPRM, continuous control monitoring) and a clear point of view on where AI augments versus replaces human judgement in program delivery

A strong communicator and problem-solver who balances persuasion with active listening, possesses exceptional stakeholder management skills to engage with engineering leaders and executives, and utilizes proven project management techniques to drive results.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of hands-on experience in equivalent security roles, such as Security Delivery Manager, Information Security Officer, or Threat Intelligence Program Manager

Relevant professional certifications such as Certified Information Systems Security Professional, Certified Information Security Manager, Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control, CompTIA Cybersecurity Analyst or Certified Fraud Examiner
Exposure to AI governance, model risk, or responsible-AI program work
Knowledge of privacy legislation and regulations such as HIPAA and GDPR
Experience working with security and risk tooling in cloud infrastructure, hosting, and platform contexts

scope.surface: Security Strategy and Governance (SSG) team within Global Security Services (GSS); cross-functional programs spanning third-party risk, compliance and audit readiness, AI governance, and risk and control framework rollouts
primary_metrics_inferred: OKR alignment and delivery outcomes for GSS programs
Security KPIs and monthly KPI updates
core_responsibilities: Lead program delivery for GSS's most complex initiatives — including third-party risk, compliance and audit readiness, AI governance, and the data-driven cyber risk and control framework
Set and continuously refine the program rhythm: planning cycles, status reporting, OKR alignment, and decision logs
Apply AI-enabled GSS tooling to reduce manual toil and improve the timeliness and signal of program reporting
Identify automation and AI opportunities in program management itself
Run risk analysis, contingency planning, and trade-off conversations with senior stakeholders
Maintain authoritative status materials for GSS leadership team, monthly KPI updates, and quarterly Board contributions
Act as a player-coach to other program managers across GSS and GTS
Flex into other GSS functions where strategic initiatives need senior program leadership

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear reporting line (Head of Security Strategy and Governance) and team context
Well-defined program surface and cross-functional partners listed
Mentions AI tooling and automation as genuine program priorities, not just buzzwords
Travel requirement explicitly scoped (up to 10%) and described as advance-coordinated
red_flags: Certifications (PMP, PRINCE2, ITIL, COBIT, ISO 27001) described as 'expected at this level' — treated as hard requirement but may be flexible in practice; flagged as ambiguity
Location is ambiguous: salary range mentions 'various U.S. geographic markets' suggesting US-based but no specific cities or remote/hybrid policy is explicitly stated for this role
Visa sponsorship not addressed anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + player-coach language)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — ecommerce/marketing SaaS)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed as metrics in JD)
location.type (inferred as hybrid from 10% travel requirement and US salary bands; no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite label in JD body)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from geo-based salary range language implying US locations but no office requirement stated)
ambiguities: Certifications (PMP, PRINCE2, ITIL, COBIT, ISO 27001) are described as 'expected at this level', which reads as a hard requirement, but the opening paragraph encourages close-but-not-exact candidates to apply. Classified as hard severity with bridgeable: maybe.

Location type is unclear: the JD does not explicitly state remote, hybrid, or onsite. The geo-based salary language implies US-only but no office attendance requirement is mentioned aside from up to 10% travel. Treated as hybrid/remote-flexible.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to unclear.
